transcript.clean_text: the first serious mountain test of this jura d'italia would come on italian soil the race having left hungary with an early rest day following mark kamadish's victory in balaton food the maximum picking up his 16th gear d'italia stage win first in nine years and mature fonder pool a third mallyaroza all three days in hungary for him leading the race somal detna had a fourth visit in six years to sicily visit number 20 out of the 105 gdd tally we've had beginning today in avola home of wine grapes and almonds heading up to the mighty mountain only mount tader is bigger in europe and north africa in terms of active or dormant volcanoes finishing with the ascent from nikolasi up to the fuji sapienza actually to create a brand new climb a long one 22.8 kilometers classified over 30 k's once you begin climbing full stop the race began with a very big fight for the breakaway and with big bad news for astana kazakhstan lopez climbing off his bike a left hip injury meaning that he could no longer continue the jitter the italia the second time in three years he's had to leave the race in sicily machiavelli was trying to get in the break in a bid to defend his pink jersey his teammate did get there in the end stefan oldani one of 14 riders up the road the fourteenth and last man to join was lilian calmerjan the frenchman having won at the tour in the welter looking to add a judo stage to his list of honours the group would get almost 12 minutes at one point but it'd be quickly controlled by bahrain and ineos grenadiers they still looked set for victory from the breakaway however stefano oldani becoming the first man to attack right at the bottom of the climb he take the bonus seconds at the second intermediate sprint and behind his teammate would decide not to go all in to defend the majarosa choosing to save this energy and join the altered boss so he was off to find the groupeto juanpe lopez was looking for the malaya rosa the 14-man group behind her being whittled down to six aldani the only man further up the road he was soon caught and passed just as quickly by the young man from southern spain ineos grenadiers went to work inside the last 15 kilometers of soap and tom dumulia became the first victim of the big gc names to move off the back judo the italian winner five years previous would not be in the fight for the general classification leonard kenner would be in the fight for the stage win vincenzo nebali distanced on a horrible day for astana kazakhstan that's still with around five kilometers to the top with just under three kilometers to go juan pelopez was caught by leonard kemna a quick chat maybe you take the stage i'll take the jersey something like that and they try to ride together to the finish a nervy moment inside the final few hundred meters almost had them both down but it had leonard kemna definitely sprinting away and moving to a stage win at the judo just as he'd done at the tour two years previous a second grand tour stage victory for the german lopez banging his bars but that would soon turn to the light as he'd move into the two and a half minutes down the gc favorites came in richard karapas trying to steal a second or two but this lot would all come in on the same time dominical ports of evo losing a little bit of time all the glory to borrow who had three men in that front gc group as well as stage winner kemna up the road that disappointment then turning to this delight for juante lopez giro d'italia debut had a pink jersey as the brand new leader he finished on the same time as leonard kendall the stage winner rain tara may finish third with monique and savannah and lemre's of the top six then the gc favorites but they're behind this man at the moment juan pedro lopez pulling on the malaya rosa and trek segafredo having the race lead it's a 39 second lead for lopez ahead of kemna tana may at 58 seconds yates still the best of the general classification contenders despite a bang to the knee and then seconds between all the big names stage five should be one for the sprinters but there's a biggish climb in the middle up to portilla mandrasi similar approach to a similar stage won by arnold de mar a few years ago villafranca tirena was the destination then but 30 kilometers up the road vincenzo nibali's hometown of messina the destination 174 kilometers for stage five of the jira d'italia the second of the two days in sicily before the race heads definitively to the peninsula what's around the corner well anything at this jira italia with a new stage winner and a brand new leader join us on stage five live on gcm plus discovery plus and eurosport to find out
